scope_note: QA Reactive Power Versor Coupling cert [305]. Primary sources: Hardy, G.H. and Wright, E.M. (2008) An Introduction to the Theory of Numbers, Oxford, ISBN 978-0-19-921986-5, Ch.X; Wildberger, N.J. (2005) Divine Proportions: Rational Trigonometry, Wild Egg Books, ISBN 978-0-9757492-0-8 (spread/quadrance replacing sin^2/cos^2); Hestenes, D. and Sobczyk, G. (1984) Clifford Algebra to Geometric Calculus, Reidel, ISBN 978-90-277-1673-6 (versor grading). Rational trigonometry is used throughout the observer layer because Wildberger spread/quadrance produces exact Fraction values from A1 states; hyperbolic trig and standard sin/cos are excluded (hyperbolic is for blue chromogeometric layer, not Euclidean power systems; sin/cos produce transcendentals). CLAIM: (C1) Grade-reactive isomorphism: det(M^k)=(-1)^k (Cassini, cert [299]); odd k=reactive operator (det=-1), even k=active (det=+1); verified k=0..24. (C2) Rational spread = QA power factor: s(b,e)=e*e/(b*b+e*e) in (0,1)∩Q for all (b,e) in {1..9}^2; A1 guarantees strict interior; (1-s)+s=1 exactly (Fraction); active fraction 1-s=b*b/(b*b+e*e); replaces sin^2(phi) in observer layer. (C3) 45-degree locus: s(k,k)=1/2 exactly for all k=1..9 (b=e diagonal); Singularity (9,9) has s=1/2; Satellite spreads={1/10,1/5,4/13,1/2,9/13,4/5,9/10} closed under s->1-s. (C4) Reactive complement: s(e,b)=1-s(b,e) exactly for all (b,e); Cosmos closed under (b,e)->(e,b) (gcd(b,e)=gcd(e,b)); min Cosmos spread + max Cosmos spread = 1 (at (9,1) and (1,9)). (C5) T-step cross-spread: cs=(b*d-e*e)^2/(G*G') where d=A1_mod(b+e,9), G=b*b+e*e, G'=e*e+d*d; exact Fraction in [0,1] for all 72 Cosmos states; Singularity cross-spread=0 (self-coupling). Builds on cert [291] (Cosmos period 24), cert [296] (det(M)=-1 odd-grade), cert [299] (Cassini det(M^k)=(-1)^k), cert [303] (Cosmos triads), cert [304] (polyphase sums). Lineage: derived 2026-06-02.
